skill.name: Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporter for Java
skill.description: A skill for exporting OpenTelemetry traces, metrics, and logs to Azure Monitor and Application Insights using Java. Provides code examples for setting up the deprecated exporter and migrating to the new autoconfigure package.
skill.summary: Export OpenTelemetry telemetry data to Azure Monitor from Java applications
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill containing code examples for using the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporter library. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 security issues. The skill provides informational content about a deprecated Microsoft library with migration guidance. No executable code, network calls, or file system access is present in the skill itself.

content.user_title: Export OpenTelemetry Data to Azure Monitor
content.value_statement: This skill helps Java developers export OpenTelemetry traces, metrics, and logs to Azure Monitor and Application Insights. It provides code examples for the deprecated exporter package and guidance for migrating to the recommended autoconfigure solution.
actual_capabilities: Generate code for configuring Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporter in Java applications
Provide examples for creating and managing OpenTelemetry spans with Azure export
Show how to add custom span attributes and record exceptions
Explain migration from deprecated exporter to autoconfigure package
Demonstrate setting up metrics and histograms with Azure Monitor
Guide on configuring connection strings and environment variables
limitations: The exporter package is deprecated - skill includes migration guidance
Does not provide runtime execution or actual Azure connectivity
Requires Java 8 or higher and Maven/Gradle for dependency management
No support for older Java versions (pre-8)

use_cases.title: Set Up Azure Monitoring for Java Apps
use_cases.description: Configure OpenTelemetry to export traces and metrics from a Java application to Azure Monitor for centralized observability
use_cases.target_user: Java developers building cloud-native applications on Azure

use_cases.title: Migrate from Deprecated Exporter
use_cases.description: Move from the deprecated azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter to the new azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure package
use_cases.target_user: Developers currently using the deprecated exporter library

use_cases.title: Implement Distributed Tracing
use_cases.description: Create spans, nested spans, and record exceptions to trace request flows across microservices in Azure
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers and backend developers implementing observability

prompt_templates.title: Basic Azure Monitor Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to set up Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporter in my Java application. I want to export traces to Application Insights using the connection string.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Azure monitoring in Java

prompt_templates.title: Create Custom Spans
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I create OpenTelemetry spans with custom attributes in Java and export them to Azure Monitor? Include examples of setting attributes and recording exceptions.

prompt_templates.title: Add Custom Span Processor
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a custom SpanProcessor that adds custom attributes to every span before exporting to Azure Monitor. Show how to register it with the OpenTelemetry SDK.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding custom context to all spans

prompt_templates.title: Migrate to Autoconfigure
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the steps to migrate from the deprecated azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter to azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure? Show the dependency changes and code updates needed.
prompt_templates.scenario: Upgrading to the new autoconfigure package

output_examples.input: Show me how to set up Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporter in my Java application
output_examples.output: To set up Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporter in Java, add the autoconfigure dependency to your pom.xml and configure the connection string. The recommended approach uses azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure instead of the deprecated exporter. Set the APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING environment variable and initialize the SDK using AutoConfiguredOpenTelemetrySdk.builder().

output_examples.input: How do I create spans with custom attributes for Azure Monitor?
output_examples.output: Get a Tracer from OpenTelemetry, create a Span using tracer.spanBuilder(), set attributes with setAttribute(), and ensure you call span.end() in a finally block. Use try-with-resources with makeCurrent() for proper context propagation. Record exceptions using span.recordException() for error tracking in Application Insights.

best_practices: Always use try-with-resources with span.makeCurrent() for proper context management
Record exceptions on spans using recordException() for error tracking in Azure
Follow OpenTelemetry semantic conventions for attribute naming
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode connection strings in source code - use environment variables
Avoid creating spans without ending them - always call span.end()
Do not skip exception recording - always call recordException() in catch blocks

faq.question: Is this package still supported?
faq.answer: No, azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter is deprecated. Use azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure instead.

faq.question: What Java version is required?
faq.answer: Java 8 or higher is required for the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry packages.

faq.question: How do I get the connection string?
faq.answer: Get your connection string from the Azure Portal under Application Insights > Configure > Connection Strings.

faq.question: Can I use this with Spring Boot?
faq.answer: Yes, the autoconfigure package integrates with Spring Boot auto-configuration. Add the dependency and configure the connection string.

faq.question: Does this support metrics export?
faq.answer: Yes, OpenTelemetry metrics can be exported to Azure Monitor. Use Meter to create counters and histograms.

faq.question: What is the difference between exporter and autoconfigure?
faq.answer: The exporter requires manual setup while autoconfigure provides automatic instrumentation for common frameworks and simplified configuration.
